commentaries.author: Ephrem The Syrian
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Therefore I have profaned the princes of the sanctuary, and have given Jacob to the curse, and Israel to reproaches. “Your princes profaned the sanctuary. ” Those who were from the house of Manasseh profaned the sanctuary with the four-faced idol, which they placed inside the sanctuary. Because of those crimes, and in particular because of Manasseh’s crime, “I have given Jacob to the curse, and Israel to reviling.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Profaned, or declared such, (Haydock) Nadab, (Leviticus x. 1.; Calmet) or Moses and Aaron. (Menochius)
Septuagint, "and the princes defiled my holy things. "(Haydock)
Slaughter. Hebrew, "anathema. "Yet I will re-establish all. (Calmet)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
